Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals marked proteinuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine color/appearance, further supporting possible renal dysfunction. Hemoglobin A1c is low, but current glucose is normal. No evidence of infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for possible acute or chronic kidney disease and further assessment of underlying etiology.
